title: WP3 — Characterisation & Dexterous Manipulation
subtitle: George Jenkinson · Research Associate · University of Bath. Living progress page — updated continuously.
footer: Access-restricted progress page · ARIA WP3 · University of Bath · not indexed, not for redistribution.
overview_aim: Two threads within WP3: a methodology that characterises and classifies soft actuators (primary milestone D3.1), and an exploratory wave-based manipulation framework relevant to WP3.2's dimensionality-reduction aim and Milestone 5 (internal physical interactions).

groups.heading: WP3.1 / D3.1 — Characteristics Classification Methodology
groups.aim: A reusable pipeline that fingerprints an actuator's controllability/observability and places it in a shared, interpretable feature space.

groups.heading: WP3.2 (relevance) — Wave-Based In-Hand Manipulation
groups.aim: Author a manipulation in a low-dimensional "manifold" (intent) space, then enact it on any capable manipulator — a tangible instance of the dimensionality-reduction idea, expanding Milestone 5's geometric-factors theme.

groups.heading: Outputs & Reusable Tooling
groups.aim: The software is a deliverable in its own right, and much of it is portable to other projects.
